mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, it signals weakening labor demand and recession risk, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of months where the unemployment rate crosses above the 5.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based consumer demand contraction, leading to negative corporate earnings revisions and lower equity prices over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal shows no predictive power (p > 0.10) compared to single-series models in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month drops in industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following the industrial production trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity multiples, driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests and fails to predict negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth (Rank IC < 0.01) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5% signals economic stagnation that reduces corporate profit expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of quarters where GDP growth falls below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production falls while employment rises indicates rising labor costs without output gains, squeezing corporate margins and triggering short-term equity declines.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 5-day returns with accuracy above 52% in walk-forward validation.
